facts: NIH ChestX-ray14 admitted (Bethesda, 112k images); CheXpert (Stanford, 224k) and PadChest (Alicante ES, 161k) form-gated and deferred to batch-2 access. Pair definition exists for future materialization.
hypotheses: Three-way transitivity NIH→CheXpert→PadChest holds via composition rather than each pairing being an independent shift.
The label-pipeline shift is dominant over the population shift.
Foundation-model performance on NIH exposes a label-signature distinct from the image-signature.
intuitions: Multi-cohort foundation-model evaluations 2024-25 (CXR-Foundation, MedImageInsight, BioViL) on the triad likely exist.
MIMIC-CXR likely appears as a fourth leg in external work, though it is excluded from this corpus.
unknowns: Whether radiologist-disagreement is separable from label-noise cannot be determined because neither dataset exposes annotator agreement structure.
